### Example 4: Local Development Server Initializationn
The exact command from the README to start local development:
```
# This single command starts the entire Inconvo platform locally
npx inconvo@latest dev
# Expected output:
# ✔ Inconvo Engine started on port 26686
# ✔ Dashboard available at http://localhost:26686
# ✔ API documentation at http://localhost:26686/docs
# ✔ Press Ctrl+C to stop
```
**What happens**: The CLI downloads the Inconvo engine, starts the query validation service, permission manager, and observability dashboard.
It auto-discovers local databases and suggests schema configurations.
The `@latest` tag ensures you always run the newest stable version.

# Rails console doesn't use DATABASE_URL environment variable · Issue #19256 · rails/rails
created: 2015-03-08, last updated: 2026-04-16, [Read more](https://github.com/rails/rails/issues/19256)

In irb I can run `ENV['DATABASE_URL']` and get the correct URL so I know the environment variable is correctly set.
This rails guide seems to indicate that database.yml is optional and indeed I can run a server with `rails s` even with no database.yml present.
...
I had to run my command with `foreman start` which sources the env before the process gets run
``` $ rm config/database.yml $ foreman run bundle exec rails runner 'puts ENV["DATABASE_URL"].present?'
...
You can definitely run rails without a `database.yml`.|
...
```
export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@localhost/database"
echo $DATABASE_URL
# postgresql://user:password@localhost/database
bundle exec echo $DATABASE_URL
# postgresql://user:password@localhost/database
irb
> ENV['DATABASE_URL']
...
bundle exec irb
> ENV['DATABASE_URL']
# postgresql://user:password@localhost/database
bundle exec rails runner ''puts ENV["DATABASE_URL"]'
...
$ bundle exec rails -v
Rails 4.2.0
# This time database.yml is present (but with values I would like to override)
$ bundle exec rails c

# GitHub - glb/pop
created: 2018-03-07, last updated: 2026-04-17, [Read more](https://github.com/glb/pop)

Pop is easily configured using a YAML file.
The configuration file should be stored in `config/database.yml` or `database.yml`.
...
```
...
- `env` - This function will look for the named environment variable and insert it into your file.
This is useful for configuring production databases without having to store secret information in your repository.
`{{ env "DATABASE_URL" }}`
...
If the variable can not be found a default value will be used.
`{{ envOr "MYSQL_HOST" "localhost" }}`
You can generate a default configuration file using the `init` command:
```
$ soda g config
```
The default will generate a `database.yml` file in the current directory for a PostgreSQL database.
You can override the type of database using the `-t` flag and passing in any of the supported database types: `postgres`, `cockroach`, `mysql`, or `sqlite3`.
...
Assuming you defined a configuration file like that described in the above section you can automatically create those databases using the `soda` command:
#### Create All Databases
```
$ soda create -a
```
#### Create a Specific Database
```
$ soda create -e development
```

Now that your connection parameters are setup, Doctrine can create the

# Set the DATABASE_URL environment variable in GitHub CI
created: 2020-09-22, last updated: 2025-10-22, [Read more](https://github.com/cookiecutter/cookiecutter-django/issues/2848)

It's the `Entrypoint` script at `./compose/production/django/entrypoint` that is exporting the `DATABASE_URL` to the `Root` user's session for the `django` service.
The `Entrypoint` script is run **only** at container creation time at which point all the required environment variables are created and exported.
...
I would suggest that instead of using `run` to run pytest you could simply do something like this in the `ci.yml` file:
```
name: CI
# Enable Buildkit and let compose use it to speed up image building
env:
DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1
COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD: 1
# create a top-level GitHub action env key that will be accessible to every GitHub action step and job
DATABASE_URL: postgres://.......
......
- name: Run Django Tests (passing DATABASE_URL as an env var to exec)
run: docker-compose -f local.yml exec -e DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL} -T django pytest
......
```

part.text:
**Testing Initialization Commands**

I'm exploring different initialization command variations. Current focus is on finding the precise command to initiate inconvo, experimenting with `npx inconvo@latest dev init` and `npx inconvo@latest model init` after initial searches suggested `npx inconvo@latest dev`.

**Investigating Initialization Options**

I'm digging deeper into initialization commands. After noting `dev configure`, I'm considering if `inconvo.yaml` itself is the key, possibly creating it manually or via another command. I'm exploring `npx inconvo@latest model pull` as a potential path to generate directory structures, even with a placeholder agent ID, and re-evaluating `npx inconvo@latest init` if a different package exists.

**Examining Development Flags**

I'm re-examining the output of `npx inconvo@latest dev --help`. It seems `dev configure` might be the closest command, though it's interactive. I'm considering manually crafting `inconvo.yaml` with default values, as the project requires a valid file. I'll also investigate the potential for `npx inconvo@latest dev` to create necessary structures.

# Inconvo
created: 2022-02-25, last updated: 2026-04-16, [Read more](https://github.com/inconvo)

# InvokeAI/docs/configuration.md at main · invoke-ai/InvokeAI
created: 2022-08-17, last updated: 2026-03-21, [Read more](https://github.com/invoke-ai/InvokeAI/blob/main/docs/configuration.md)

Runtime settings, including the location of files and
directories, memory usage, and performance, are managed via the
`invokeai.yaml` config file or environment variables.
A subset
of settings may be set via commandline arguments.
Settings sources are used in this order:
- CLI args
- Environment variables
- `invokeai.yaml` settings
- Fallback: defaults
...
On startup, InvokeAI searches for its "root" directory.
This is the directory
that contains models, images, the database, and so on.
It also contains
a configuration file called `invokeai.yaml`.
...
Inside the root directory, we read settings from the `invokeai.yaml` file.
It has two sections - one for internal use and one for user settings:
...
You can use any config file with the `--config` CLI arg.
Pass in the path to the `invokeai.yaml` file you want to use.

Title: CLI Overview

URL Source: https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/

Markdown Content:
# CLI Overview | Inconvo
[Skip to content](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/#_top)

[![Image 1](https://inconvo.com/_astro/favicon.CsakJfV6.svg)![Image 2](https://inconvo.com/_astro/favicon-light.C3IfgQMB.svg) Inconvo](https://inconvo.com/)[Documentation](https://inconvo.com/docs/getting-started/)[Connections](https://inconvo.com/docs/guides/connect/postgresql/)[API Reference](https://inconvo.com/docs/api-reference/introduction/)[CLI](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/)[Examples](https://inconvo.com/docs/examples/)[Ask an expert](https://inconvo.com/demo/)[Get Started](https://auth.inconvo.ai/en/login)

Toggle Menu

Search Ctrl K

 Cancel 

[GitHub](https://github.com/inconvoai/inconvo)

Toggle theme

[Introduction](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli)

#### Commands

[Model](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/model)[Connection](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/connection)

Docs

CLI Overview

# CLI Overview

Install, authenticate, and use the Inconvo CLI to manage your agents and semantic models.

The Inconvo CLI lets you manage semantic models and database connections directly from your terminal. It’s designed to work alongside coding agents like Claude Code and Cursor.

## Installation

[Section titled “Installation”](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/#installation)

Terminal window

`npm install -g @inconvoai/cli`

Or use it via `npx` without installing globally:

Terminal window

`npx inconvo --help`

## Authentication

[Section titled “Authentication”](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/#authentication)

The CLI uses your Inconvo API key for authentication. Set it as an environment variable:

Terminal window

`export INCONVO_API_KEY=your_api_key_here`

Or pass it directly to any command:

Terminal window

`npx inconvo model pull --api-key your_api_key_here`

## Command Reference

[Section titled “Command Reference”](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/#command-reference)

| Command | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `inconvo model pull` | Pull semantic model files into `.inconvo/` for one or more agents |
| `inconvo model agent list` | List agents available in your organisation |
| `inconvo model action run` | Run a model action from a raw JSON payload |
| `inconvo model action schema` | List supported model actions and metadata |
| `inconvo model table` | Manage table settings (queryable, joinable, off) |
| `inconvo model column` | Manage column settings (on/off) |
| `inconvo model computed` | Manage computed columns |
| `inconvo model relation` | Manage relations between tables |
| `inconvo model condition` | Manage conditions on tables |
| `inconvo model policy` | Manage policies |
| `inconvo model virtual` | Manage virtual tables |
| `inconvo model user-context` | Manage user context configuration |
| `inconvo connection get` | Show metadata for a connection |
| `inconvo connection list` | List connections for an agent |
| `inconvo connection sync` | Trigger a full database resync for a connection |
| `inconvo connection update` | Update a connection description |

## Typical Workflow

[Section titled “Typical Workflow”](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/#typical-workflow)

Terminal window

```
# 1. Pull your semantic modelnpx inconvo model pull
# 2. Make changes via your coding agent or the mutation commands# 3. Sync changes backnpx inconvo connection sync --agent agt_123
```

For detailed documentation on each command group, see:

[Model Commands](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/model)Pull, inspect, and mutate your agent's semantic model.

[Connection Commands](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/connection)Manage database connections and trigger resyncs.

[Supabase](https://inconvo.com/docs/guides/connect/supabase)[Model](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/model)

*   [Overview](https://inconvo.com/docs/cli/#_top)

**Discovering Command Functionality**

I've been probing the documentation and noticed the absence of an explicit `init` command. This is interesting given the CLI's stated purpose of managing semantic models and database connections directly. I'm continuing to explore its capabilities.

**Analyzing Configuration Paths**

I'm currently processing how `inconvo dev` handles its configuration directory. My understanding is that it defaults to `~/.inconvo`, but I need to reconcile this with the user's specified project initialization path, `/home/user/myproject`, and how model files are pulled into `.inconvo/` within the project context.

content.text:
# npx
created: 2020-10-16, last updated: 2026-05-13, [Read more](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v7/commands/npx/)

To run a binary *other than* the named binary, specify one or more `--package` options, which will prevent npm from inferring the package from the first command argument.
...
When run via the `npx` binary, all flags and options *must* be set prior to any positional arguments.
When run via `npm exec`, a double-hyphen `--` flag can be used to suppress npm's parsing of switches and options that should be sent to the executed command.
For example:
`$ npx foo@latest bar --package=@npmcli/foo`
...
The double-hyphen character is recommended to explicitly tell npm to stop parsing command line options and switches.
The following command would thus be equivalent to the `npx` command above:
`$ npm exec -- foo@latest bar --package=@npmcli/foo`
...
Run the version of `tap` in the local dependencies, with the provided arguments:
...
```
$ npm x -c 'eslint && say "hooray, lint passed"'
$ npx -c 'eslint && say "hooray, lint passed"'

# npm-init | npm Docs
created: 2022-10-26, last updated: 2026-05-13, [Read more](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v8/commands/npm-init/)

npm init <package-spec> (same as `npx <package-spec>)
npm init <@scope> (same as `npx <@scope>/create`)
aliases: create, innit
...
If the initializer is omitted (by just calling `npm init`), init will fall back to legacy init behavior.
...
If you pass `--scope`, it will create a scoped package.
*Note:* if a user already has the `create-<initializer>` package globally installed, that will be what `npm init` uses.
If you want npm to use the latest version, or another specific version you must specify it:
- `npm init foo@latest` # fetches and runs the latest `create-foo` from the registry
- `npm init foo@1.2.3` # runs `create-foo@1.2.3` specifically
### Forwarding additional options
...
This will cause `@mycorp` to be mapped to the registry for future installation of packages specified according to the pattern `@mycorp/package`.
...
# accept all defaults, and create a package named "@foo/whatever",
# instead of just named "whatever"
npm init --scope=@foo --yes
```

# How to use npx
created: 2021-02-06, last updated: 2026-05-09, [Read more](https://zenn.dev/ryuu/articles/what-npxcommand)

```
$ npx nuxi@latest init <project-name>
```
という記述が目に入りました。
...
もし`npx`が存在しない場合は以下のコマンドでグローバルにインストールできます。
```
$ npm install -g npx
```
...
$ npm init <project-name>
...
`npx`を使うことでインストールされていないコマンドであっても自動的に探してインストール、実行まで行ってくれます。
...
npxコマンドの基本的な使い方は次のとおりです。
**インストール済みモジュールの実行**
```
$ npx <インストール済みモジュール名>
```
**インストールしていないモジュールを実行（実行後に自動削除）**
```
$ npx <未インストールのモジュール名>
```
**GitHub上の特定リポジトリを明示的に指定して実行**
```
$ npx github:<リポジトリ名>
```

# npm-init - man pages section 1: User Commands
created: 2022-07-28, last updated: 2025-10-02, [Read more](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E88353_01/html/E37839/npm-init-1.html)

npm-init - Create a package.json file
Synopsis
npm init [--force|-f|--yes|-y|--scope]
npm init <@scope> (same as `npx <@scope>/create`)
npm init [<@scope>/]<name> (same as `npx [<@scope>/]create-<name>`)
...
The init command is transformed to a corresponding npm exec operation
as follows:
o npm init foo -> npm exec create-foo
o npm init @usr/foo -> npm exec @usr/create-foo
...
Note: if a user already has the create-<initializer> package globally
installed, that will be what npm init uses.
If you want npm to use the
latest version, or another specific version you must specify it:
o npm init foo@latest # fetches and runs the latest create-foo from
the registry
o npm init foo@1.2.3 # runs create-foo@1.2.3 specifically
Forwarding additional options
Any additional options will be passed directly to the command, so npm
init foo -- --hello will map to npm exec -- create-foo --hello.
...
$ npm init -w packages/a

# npm-init - Docs4dev
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-03-19, [Read more](https://www.docs4dev.com/docs/npm/latest/cli/v10/commands/npm-init.html)

```
npm init <package-spec> (same as `npx <package-spec>`)
npm init <@scope> (same as `npx <@scope>/create`)
aliases: create, innit
```
...
The init command is transformed to a corresponding `npm exec` operation as follows:
- `npm init foo` -> `npm exec create-foo`
...
If you pass `--scope`, it will create a scoped package.
*Note:* if a user already has the `create-<initializer>` package globally installed, that will be what `npm init` uses.
If you want npm to use the latest version, or another specific version you must specify it:
- `npm init foo@latest` # fetches and runs the latest `create-foo` from the registry
- `npm init foo@1.2.3` # runs `create-foo@1.2.3` specifically
#### Forwarding additional options
...
```
$ npm init react-app ./my-react-app
```
...
```
...
```
# accept all defaults, and create a package named "@foo/whatever",
# instead of just named "whatever"
npm init --scope=@foo --yes
```

# 버전관련 - 인프런 | 커뮤니티 질문&답변
created: 2025-05-26, last updated: 2025-09-23, [Read more](https://www.inflearn.com/community/questions/1601693/%EB%B2%84%EC%A0%84%EA%B4%80%EB%A0%A8)

웹사이트에서 보신 명령어는 npx @react-native-community/cli@latest init AwesomeProject인데, 이는 최신 버전의 React Native CLI를 사용하여 프로젝트를 초기화하는 명령어입니다.
...
일반적으로, 강의에서 사용하는 명령어는 다음과 같습니다:
npx react-native init MatzipApp
@latest
현재 상태에서는 두 가지 접근 방식을 모두 사용할 수 있습니다.
npx react-native-cli@latest init YourAppName으로 최신 버전의 CLI를 활용하여 프로젝트를 생성하되, 강의와의 차이를 비교해보는 것도 좋은 학습법입니다.
npx react-native-cli@latest init YourAppName
...
`npx react-native init MatzipApp`: 기본적으로 React Native CLI를 통해 새로운 React Native 프로젝트를 초기화하는 명령어입니다.
최신 버전의 CLI를 사용하려고 할 때는
`@latest`옵션을 생략해도 됩니다.
...
`npx react-native-cli@latest init YourAppName`으로 최신 버전의 CLI를 활용하여 프로젝트를 생성하되, 강의와의 차이를 비교해보는 것도 좋은 학습법입니다.

# 자바스크립트 프로젝트 생성법 정리 (npm init, npm create, npx)
created: 2023-10-30, last updated: 2026-04-29, [Read more](https://daleseo.com/js-npm-init/)

```
npm init ???
```
라고 실행하면 npm은 패키지 저장소에서
...
그러므로,
```
npm init ???
```
또는
```
npm create ???
```
를 실행하는 것은
```
npx create-???
```
를 실행하는 것과 동일합니다.
예를 들어서,
```
npm init gatsby
```
를 실행하는 것은
```
npx create-gatsby
```
를 실행하는 것과 마찬가지이고,
```
npm create vue
```
를 실행하는 것은
```
npx create-vue
```
...
여기서
```
@latest
```
는 명령어를 실행하는 시점에 npm 저장소에 올라와 있는 해당 패키지의 최신 버전을 가리킵니다.
즉, 동일하게
```
npm init ???@latest
```
를 실행하더라도, 어제랑 오늘, 내일 설치되는 버전이 다를 수 있는 것이지요.
...
```
...
이 것이 바로 많은 프레임워크 문서에서 패키지 이름 뒤에
```
@latest
```
를 붙이도록 권장하고 있는 이유입니다.

# Quickstart with Nx
created: undefined, last updated: 2026-05-06, [Read more](https://nx.dev/getting-started)

### Install the Nx CLI
Installing Nx globally is **optional** - you can use `npx` to run Nx commands without installing it globally, especially if you're working with Node.js projects.
However, if you prefer a global installation, here are some options:
❯
npm add --global nx
**Note:** You can also use Yarn, pnpm, or Bun
2
### Start fresh or add to existing project
For JavaScript-based projects you can **start with a new workspace** using the following command:
❯
npx create-nx-workspace@latest
**Add to an existing project: (recommended also for non-JS projects)**
❯
npx nx@latest init
**Get the complete experience:** For a fully integrated development workflow with AI-powered CI features, start directly from Nx Cloud.
